server.committeeCodeInvalid: Enter the 6-character committee code (letters and digits only, e.g. ECO741). Ask your chair or check SMT -> Room codes.
server.enterConferenceFirst: Enter your conference code first (go back to the previous step).
server.committeeCodeNoMatch: No committee matches that code for this event. Enter the conference code first, then the exact 6-character committee code (SMT -> Room codes or Supabase -> conferences).
server.committeeCodeMustBeSix: Committee code must be exactly 6 letters or digits (e.g. ECO741).
server.onlyStaffSetCode: Only chairs, SMT, and admins can set committee codes.
server.chairSeatOnly: You can only set the committee code for a session where you have a seat.

documents.positionPaperGuideBlurb: Download the placeholder guide while the final PDF is being prepared.
documents.delegatePositionPaperNote: Position papers are uploaded by chairs with feedback.
documents.googleDocsHelp: Use New Google Doc to open Google in a new tab, then paste the doc link here. Plain text below is optional. Every document needs either local text, a Google Doc link, or both.

voting.helpRequiredMajorityBody: Choose the vote threshold used to decide pass/fail. `Simple` is more than half. `2/3` requires a supermajority.
voting.helpMustVoteBody: Marks this motion as a “must-vote” item. This is used to communicate the requirement to participants (depending on your committee’s setup).
voting.delegateBlurb: The dais records votes in session (e.g. roll call). Motion votes here follow Yes/No only (abstain is not used). You can follow the tally; delegates do not cast votes in the app.
voting.noSeatedDelegates: No seated delegates available for this motion.

profile.name: Name
profile.customPronounNote: Your profile had a custom pronoun set that is no longer accepted here. Pick an option above (or leave blank). Saving replaces the old value.
profile.errPronounsPreset: Choose a pronoun from the list.
profile.errStorageSetup: Profile picture storage is not set up. In the Supabase project, run migration 00100_profile_pictures_storage_bucket.sql, or create a public storage bucket named profile-pictures (see README).
profile.errPublicUrl: Could not resolve public URL for uploaded image.

rop.title: Rules of Procedure (RoP)
rop.content:
# Rules of Procedure

## Points and Motions
- **Point of Order**: Correct procedure
- **Point of Information**: Question to speaker
- **Point of Personal Privilege**: Personal comfort
- **Motion to Table**: Postpone debate
- **Motion to Adjourn**: End session

## Voting
- Simple majority for procedural matters
- 2/3 majority for substantive matters
- Roll-call vote if requested

examples.title: Examples
examples.content:
# Examples

## Resolution Format
```
The General Assembly,
...
1. Calls upon member states to...
2. Urges the international community to...
```

## Position Paper Structure
1. Background
2. Country Policy
3. Proposed Solutions

templates.title: Templates
templates.content:
# Templates

## Chair Report Template
- Committee overview
- Key discussion points
- Resolutions passed
- Recommendations

chairReport.title: Chair Report
chairReport.content:
# Chair Report

Document committee proceedings and outcomes here.

guides.googleDocsHelp: New Google Doc opens in another tab. Paste a shareable link so delegates can open the doc here (view/edit depends on Google sharing).
guides.markdownOptional: Markdown notes (optional if you use a Google Doc)

glossary.title: MUN Agenda Glossary
glossary.subtitle: Unique terminology matched to your committee code and current agenda topics.

delegation.definition: A country or observer team representing one state or entity in committee.

placard.definition: Country sign used to request the floor, vote, and signal procedural motions.

dais.definition: Chairing team that moderates debate, applies procedure, and manages speaking order.

quorum.definition: Minimum number of delegations required to conduct formal business and votes.

motion.definition: Formal proposal to change the flow of debate, such as caucus type or speaking time.

point_of_order.definition: Intervention when procedure is being applied incorrectly under committee rules.

moderated_caucus.definition: Structured debate segment with short timed speeches on a defined subtopic.

unmoderated_caucus.definition: Informal negotiation period where delegates move freely to draft and merge ideas.

draft_resolution.definition: Working text of proposed committee action before final voting and adoption.

amendment.definition: Change proposed to draft text, either friendly or voted upon as unfriendly.

operative_clause.definition: Action-oriented numbered clause describing what the committee proposes to do.

preambulatory_clause.definition: Context-setting clause citing background principles, concerns, and prior frameworks.

right_of_reply.definition: Opportunity to respond to a direct statement that affected delegation dignity or position.

veto_power.definition: UNSC permanent-member authority to block substantive draft resolutions.

chapter_vii.definition: UN Charter authority on threats to peace, sanctions, and use-of-force measures.

sanctions_regime.definition: Coordinated restrictions used to influence behavior without direct military action.

arms_control.definition: Agreements and verification systems that limit types, quantities, or deployment of weapons.

non_proliferation.definition: Policies to prevent spread of nuclear, chemical, biological, or advanced weapon capabilities.

confidence_building_measures.definition: Transparency and communication tools that reduce misunderstanding and conflict risk.

universal_periodic_review.definition: Peer-review process where each UN member state reports and receives rights recommendations.

special_rapporteur.definition: Independent expert appointed to monitor and report on thematic or country rights issues.

treaty_body.definition: Committee of experts supervising implementation of a specific human-rights treaty.

public_health_emergency.definition: Serious outbreak event requiring urgent coordinated national and international response.

surveillance.definition: Continuous collection and analysis of health data to detect and track disease trends.

health_systems.definition: Improving workforce, financing, logistics, and governance for reliable healthcare delivery.

transnational_crime.definition: Criminal activity coordinated across borders through networks and illicit markets.

illicit_trafficking.definition: Illegal movement of drugs, arms, persons, or goods across jurisdictions.

rule_of_law_capacity.definition: Institutional ability of justice systems to investigate, prosecute, and protect rights fairly.

sustainable_development.definition: Growth model balancing social progress, economic opportunity, and environmental protection.

financing_for_development.definition: Public and private funding mechanisms that support long-term development priorities.

policy_coordination.definition: Alignment of state and agency actions to avoid duplication and increase collective impact.

child_protection.definition: Measures preventing exploitation, abuse, and violence against children in all settings.

education_access.definition: Ensuring inclusive, equitable, and continuous learning opportunities for all children.

humanitarian_response.definition: Rapid relief actions addressing urgent needs in crises and conflict settings.

gender_mainstreaming.definition: Integrating gender analysis into policy design, implementation, and evaluation.

gender_based_violence.definition: Harm directed at someone based on gender identity, roles, or unequal power relations.

economic_empowerment.definition: Expanding access to decent work, finance, ownership, and decision-making power.

source_attribution.definition: Citing information origin clearly so claims can be verified and contextualized.

verified_reporting.definition: Journalistic standard requiring corroboration before publishing contested facts.

editorial_independence.definition: Separation of reporting decisions from political pressure or stakeholder influence.

climate_mitigation.definition: Actions that reduce greenhouse-gas emissions or increase long-term carbon removal.

climate_adaptation.definition: Policies and investments that reduce vulnerability to climate-related impacts.

cyber_norms.definition: Shared expectations for responsible state behavior in digital and cyber domains.

critical_infrastructure.definition: Essential systems such as power, water, health, and communications requiring resilience.

epidemiology.definition: Study of disease patterns, causes, and distribution in populations.

preparedness_response.definition: Planning, coordination, and surge actions to contain and manage emergencies.

non_refoulement.definition: Principle prohibiting return of people to places where they face serious harm.

durable_solutions.definition: Long-term outcomes for displaced people: return, local integration, or resettlement.

nuclear_safeguards.definition: Verification measures ensuring nuclear materials are not diverted for weapons use.

counter_terrorism.definition: Integrated legal, preventive, and security measures against terrorist activity.

due_process.definition: Fair legal procedures protecting rights during investigation, prosecution, and sanctioning.

resource_security.definition: Reliable access to food, water, and energy under economic and environmental stress.

resilience.definition: Capacity of communities and institutions to absorb shocks and recover effectively.

ideas.addIntro: Add a Google Doc to draft a resolution idea in place, or type a short note (or both).
ideas.googleDocsCreateHelp: New Google Doc opens in another tab; paste the link here after it is created.

stances.heatmapHelp: To what extent does your allocation support ___? (1-5 scale)
stances.extentHelpBody: 1 means your allocation has little support for the topic, and 5 means strong support. Pick a number that matches your stance in the committee.
stances.noteLanguageWarning: Reader warning: this note may contain inappropriate language.
stances.saveHelpBody: Saves the stance note for this allocation. It can be viewed by staff and (depending on committee settings) other participants.

speeches.googleDocsHelp: Opens Google in a new tab; paste the document URL here when ready.
speeches.plainTextOptional: Plain text draft (optional if you use a Google Doc)
speeches.noSpeechesYet: No speeches yet. Link a Google Doc or paste text.
speeches.noContentHint: Link a Google Doc with Edit, or add plain text in the form.

committeeRoom.clearSearchTitle: Clear search and show all delegations
committeeRoom.searchVacantTitle: Search for "vacant" seats in the room
committeeRoom.searchHint: Type a country, delegate name, school, pronoun, or "vacant" to highlight matches in the room. Press Enter to jump to the first match; Esc clears. Click Vacant to jump to empty seats.
committeeRoom.findLabel: Find a delegation or dais member

requestToSpeak.title: Request to speak
requestToSpeak.needAllocation: You need an allocation to request to speak.
requestToSpeak.alreadyQueued: You already have an active speaking request in the queue.
requestToSpeak.requested: Requested to speak. You will appear in the queue.
requestToSpeak.helpBody: Sends your delegation to the chair queue. You can have one active waiting/current request at a time.
requestToSpeak.purposeHelpBody: This text is appended to your country name so chairs can see why you are requesting the floor.

motionVoting.help: The dais records each delegation's vote in session. Motion votes use Yes or No only (no abstain). Totals count only placards where Yes or No was recorded; absent delegations are skipped.

committeeControl.sessionNameHint: Saves automatically and appears in session history.

history.title: Session history
history.permissionsHint: Chairs can rename entries. SMT/Admin can rename and delete entries.

timerPage.title: Timer
timerPage.controlsHelp: Use General floor for overall debate timing, or Motion vote to tie visibility to a specific open vote item.
timerPage.setupHelp: Choose whether this timer is for general floor (always visible to delegates) or tied to the motion open for voting (delegates only see it while that vote is the active item). With per-speaker time on, the clock stays visible during moderated caucus regardless. Pause clock / Start clock freeze or resume the countdown. Set Speaker time (remaining) and Total time independently, then Save timer. Presets fill both to the same length; you can edit either field afterward. Pauses are logged with a short reason.
timerPage.purposeGeneralFloor: General debate / floor (not tied to a vote)
timerPage.purposeMotionVote: Open motion - timer only while that vote is active on the floor
timerPage.openMotionForVoting: Open motion (must be open for voting)
timerPage.noOpenMotionWarning: No motion is open for voting. Open a vote first, or switch purpose to general floor.
timerPage.saveToEnablePauseStart: save the timer to enable pause/start.
timerPage.pauseReasonPlaceholder: e.g. Point of order, tech issue, unmoderated caucus...
timerPage.perSpeakerHelp: Set Total time as the per-speaker cap and Speaker time as time remaining; Advance moves to the next speaker and resets remaining to the cap.
timerPage.speechNotesHelp: Notes are tied to the Current speaker timer text and, when the speaker list has someone marked Current, their delegation is stored for reference.
timerPage.noCurrentDelegation: No delegation is marked Current on the speaker list.
timerPage.remainingHelpPerSpeaker: Countdown for the current speaker (resets when you Advance).
timerPage.remainingHelpNormal: Countdown for the current speaker (can be less than total).
timerPage.totalHelpPerSpeaker: Per-speaker cap (denominator on the floor; Advance refills remaining to this).
timerPage.totalHelpNormal: Full length of this timer segment (shown after the slash on the floor).
timerPage.noPauseEvents: No pause events logged yet for this committee.
timerPage.openPauseLogHint: Open Pause log to review recorded pause reasons and timestamps.
timerPage.savedCapped: Timer saved. Speaker time was longer than total; capped to total.

chairTopicTabs.chooseTopicHelp: Choose which committee topic the floor tools control right now.
chairTopicTabs.sessionControlHint: Motions, timers, and the speaker list follow this topic row. Roll call still uses each delegate allocation.

agendaTopics.qClimateCooperation: How can member states strengthen global cooperation on climate action?
agendaTopics.qGlobalHealthcareAccess: How can the international community improve access to quality healthcare?
agendaTopics.qAiGovernance: How should states regulate AI and emerging technologies?
agendaTopics.qDisinformationImpact: What measures can reduce the global impact of disinformation?
agendaTopics.qSecurityHumanRights: How can countries balance security and human rights in counter-terrorism?
agendaTopics.qRefugeeProtection: How can the UN system support refugee protection and durable solutions?
agendaTopics.qFoodSecurity: What strategies can improve food security in climate-vulnerable regions?
agendaTopics.qNonProliferation: How should the international community prevent nuclear proliferation?

delegationNotes.title: Delegation notes
delegationNotes.concernHelpBody: Use this when your note includes a moderation-sensitive concern. The reader may see a warning, but your note will still send.
delegationNotes.sessionOnlyHint: Notes are available only during active committee sessions.
delegationNotes.unmoderatedHint: Notes are disabled during unmoderated caucus.
delegationNotes.sendHelpBody: Sends this note to the recipients selected above. It doesn’t prevent sending; it only affects how the reader is warned/flagged.
delegationNotes.noDelegations: No assigned delegations for this committee.
delegationNotes.delegateTargetsHint: Targets are limited to assigned allocations only.
delegationNotes.readerWarning: Reader warning: this note may contain inappropriate language.

errors.votingProcedure: Voting procedure is active: note composing is disabled.
errors.sessionInactive: Notes are disabled while the committee session is not active.
errors.unmoderated: Notes are disabled during unmoderated caucus.
errors.smtVerify: Verify with the staff secondary password to send notes to this committee.
errors.noRecipients: Select at least one recipient (delegation or chair).
errors.needAllocation: You need an allocation to send notes (or be a chair).

adminPage.title: Website administration
adminPage.intro: Create conference events and manage who can run the secretariat tools. Day-to-day committee oversight lives in the SMT dashboard.
adminPage.noSessionFloor: The session floor (timers, speakers, roll call) is for dais chairs only. Use SMT dashboard for live committees, or enter committee codes like delegates when you need a specific session.

adminDashboard.newEventBody: Creates a conference code (first gate) and the first committee session (second gate). Use next=/admin so you return here after setup.
adminDashboard.smtBody: Invite new SMT accounts by email, or change an existing user to delegate, chair, or SMT. Promoting someone to SMT lets them use the full secretariat dashboard during the event.
adminDashboard.inviteNeedsKey: Email invites need SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY on the server. You can still use Set role for users who already signed up.

adminStatusPortal.title: Status portal
adminStatusPortal.noSessionsYet: No committee sessions found for this event yet.
adminStatusPortal.intro: Delegates move from Pending to Confirmed after approval. Confirming provisions them as a delegate and triggers the Welcome email.

conferenceSetupPage.title: Set up a conference
conferenceSetupPage.intro: Secretariat or website admin: creates a conference code (first gate) and the first committee code (second gate). Add more committees in Supabase or via your workflow; dais chairs edit committee codes under Chair → Committee code.

conferenceSetupForm.eventNameHelp: Shown to organisers; delegates see it after they join.
conferenceSetupForm.eventCodeHelp: Everyone enters this first (whole conference). Spaces ignored; stored in capitals. Must be unique.
conferenceSetupForm.sessionNamePlaceholder: e.g. Topic or agenda title for this committee
conferenceSetupForm.sessionNameHelp: Main heading for this committee in the app header.
conferenceSetupForm.committeeHelp: Optional. Shown next to the session title.
conferenceSetupForm.committeeCodeHelp: Second gate: exactly six letters or digits, unique within this event—typically three letters from the chamber name plus three digits (e.g. ECOSOC → ECO741). Non-alphanumeric characters are stripped when you save.
conferenceSetupForm.passwordIntro: Delegates confirm allocation + this password after login. Leave blank to skip.
conferenceSetupForm.committeePasswordConfirmPlaceholder: Same as above if using a password
